text: By 2019, we will be living in a paperless society.
author: Roger Smith

didItHoldUp.analysis: Here's the punchline: paper use actually increased after computers arrived, because suddenly everyone could print everything. It peaked around 2007 and has been declining since. Digital signatures, e-tickets, online billing, and e-books have replaced a lot of paper. We're not paperless — but paper's role is dramatically diminished. COVID-19 accelerated the shift further. The prediction was directionally right but too absolute. We didn't go paperless. We went paper-light.
